primary_sources: Equal Justice Initiative (EJI) — National Memorial for Peace and Justice / Lynching in America research (aggregate documentation; individual names cross-checked via secondary lists)
Southern Poverty Law Center — Civil Rights Memorial martyrs (Black victims only) and “The Forgotten” display list
NAACP historical investigations and anti-lynching campaign documentation (e.g., Mary Turner, Emmett Till, Claude Neal, Harry & Harriette Moore)
Wikipedia “List of lynching victims in the United States” — rows explicitly marked African American / Black / Biracial (African American), with unnamed victims omitted
Veterans of the Civil Rights Movement (crmvet.org) — “41 Lives for Freedom” Civil Rights Memorial roster
notes: Accuracy-first: unnamed victims omitted; white Civil Rights Memorial martyrs omitted; Clarence Cloninger omitted (insufficient public confirmation in source text for this use).
Nat Turner included only as judicial execution after the 1831 rebellion — not as a lynching victim.
EJI documents thousands of racial terror lynchings (names on county monuments); this file is a verified named subset suitable for a memorial collage, not the complete EJI inventory.
Context phrases are brief memorial labels, not full case narratives.

Civilian killings and non-police assassinations are included when they are documented milestones or critical points in the Black story (national movement catalysis, civil rights assassinations, racial terror massacres). Actor type alone does not exclude a name.
